name: stackwarden-governance-model
purpose: Expose a client-facing deterministic governance model without leaking internal methodology names, private playbooks, or premium rule logic.
principles: Repository governance must be expressed as explicit, reviewable configuration before it becomes documentation or automation.
Every durable rule should have a source, owner, validation command, and projection or runtime consumer.
Human and AI contributors should rely on the same small deterministic commands instead of implicit team memory.
Generated or derived surfaces must be marked and freshness-checked so drift is visible.
New rules start advisory until signal quality is proven, then teams may opt into blocking enforcement.

rules.title: Governance quality contract is missing
rules.detects: No repository-local declaration of expected quality scripts or checks.
rules.recommendation: Declare the quality contract in a config-first surface such as .stackwarden/config.yml before adding more automation.

rules.title: Quality scripts are declared but missing
rules.detects: A governance config declares checks that package.json does not expose.
rules.recommendation: Add the missing scripts or update the governance source so humans and agents execute the same commands.

rules.title: Generated projection has no freshness check
rules.detects: A generated or derived document is present without an associated checker command.
rules.recommendation: Register a generator/checker pair so generated surfaces cannot silently drift.

rules.title: AI operating rules are not deterministic
rules.detects: Agent instructions are missing explicit validation commands or safety boundaries.
rules.recommendation: Add concise, command-oriented guardrails for validation, source-of-truth updates, secrets, pushes, publication, and destructive edits.

clientVocabulary.privateMethodologyPolicy: Do not expose internal methodology names in client-facing docs, findings, or package output.
